skill.name: context7
skill.description: Retrieve current documentation for software libraries and frameworks from the Context7 API using documented curl queries.
skill.summary: Look up current library documentation and API examples through Context7.
skill.author: intellectronica

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer correctly identified intentional network access and shell command examples, but the many Ruby backtick and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and ordinary prose. The skill sends library names and topic queries to context7.com through curl, so publication should include a network disclosure and guidance to encode parameters safely.

medium_findings.title: Intentional Network Requests to Context7 API
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to query context7.com with library names and topic strings. This is expected for documentation lookup, but those queries are disclosed to a third-party service and may be rate-limited.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly describes Context7 API usage and includes multiple curl commands to context7.com. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but the outbound request risk is real.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Examples Need Parameter Encoding
medium_findings.description: The curl examples require substituting library and topic values into shell commands. The skill tells users to URL-encode spaces, but unsafe interpolation of untrusted text could still alter the shell command if quotes or metacharacters are inserted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are clear and mostly quoted, so normal use is safe. The risk depends on how an agent substitutes user-provided values into the URL.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline code, and command examples. No Ruby code or hidden script execution was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and fenced bash examples, not Ruby source. The only real commands are the documented curl and jq examples.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic functions or hash usage. The cited lines contain the skill description, parameter text, and a response field description.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review found no cryptographic algorithm, checksum call, or password handling on the cited lines. The static detector matched unrelated text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound curl Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses curl to send documentation search and context requests to context7.com. This pattern requires network permission and discloses query terms to that service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The curl commands are explicit and point to a single public API domain. The pattern is intentional, not hidden.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Pipeline to jq
dangerous_patterns.description: Some examples pipe curl output into jq for JSON filtering. This is common developer tooling, but it still requires local command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pipeline is visible in the examples and has a benign parsing purpose. It is still a true external command pattern.

content.value_statement: AI assistants can rely on outdated library knowledge. This skill fetches current Context7 documentation and examples for specific libraries and topics.
actual_capabilities: Searches Context7 for a library identifier by library name and topic.
Fetches documentation snippets for a selected library and query.
Supports plain text responses for easier reading by an assistant.
Shows examples for React, Next.js, and FastAPI documentation lookups.
Uses jq examples to inspect search results and extract library IDs.
limitations: Depends on Context7 coverage and availability for each library.
Does not validate documentation against source repositories.
Does not provide an API key flow or private documentation access.

use_cases.title: Verify API Usage Before Coding
use_cases.description: Check current method names, parameters, and examples before changing application code.

use_cases.title: Update Framework Migration Notes
use_cases.description: Retrieve current routing, configuration, or lifecycle documentation while writing migration guidance.

use_cases.title: Support Debugging With Fresh References
use_cases.description: Look up relevant documentation during support investigations without relying only on training data.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Context7 to find current documentation for React useState. Summarize the key usage rules.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Framework Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Context7 to retrieve current Next.js app router documentation. Explain routing patterns and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Code Against Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Context7 to check FastAPI dependency injection documentation. Compare it with my implementation and list mismatches.

prompt_templates.title: Research Multiple Libraries
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced research for a design decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Context7 to gather current documentation for Axios interceptors and Fetch error handling. Recommend an approach for a shared HTTP client.

output_examples.input: Find current React hooks documentation for useEffect cleanup.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of current cleanup behavior, dependency guidance, and links between the selected Context7 result and the requested topic.

output_examples.input: Look up Next.js app router loading states.
output_examples.output: A focused explanation of loading UI conventions, file placement, and important constraints from the retrieved documentation.
A short implementation checklist that identifies dependency declaration patterns and expected behavior from the current docs.

best_practices: Use specific library names and narrow topic queries to improve relevance.
URL-encode query values before placing them in curl commands.
Confirm the selected library ID before fetching detailed documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not send private code, secrets, or unreleased product names as query terms.
Do not assume the first search result is correct without checking its title and description.
Do not paste untrusted text into shell commands without proper encoding.

faq.question: Does this skill require network access?
faq.answer: Yes. It queries the public Context7 API at context7.com.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: No API key is described for basic usage, but the skill notes that access is rate-limited.

faq.question: Can it retrieve private documentation?
faq.answer: No. The skill only describes public Context7 API lookups.

faq.question: What tools does it use?
faq.answer: It uses curl for API requests and jq for optional JSON filtering.

faq.question: What data is sent externally?
faq.answer: Library names, library IDs, and topic query strings are sent to Context7.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a network-use warning and guidance to encode shell parameters safely.
